mission.title: kubeelasti: helm: forward values to elasti trough env variables
mission.description:
This allows operator to work in any namespace, not only in `elasti`.
This also allows forwarding values from helm chart to elasti pods.

Had to update few third parties, as UTs & e2e didn't want to work (probably due to go1.25.1 or helm 3.18.6).

steps.title: Verify the fix
steps.description: Confirm that the issue is resolved in your environment by testing the affected functionality.

resolution.summary:
## Walkthrough Introduces a centralized config package to source operator and resolver settings from environment variables; updates operator, resolver, and Helm charts to consume these values; removes hard-coded names/ports; bumps controller-tools version; and adjusts CRD schemas and tests accordingly. ## Changes | Cohort / File(s) | Summary | | --- | --- | | **Helm: common env injection**
`charts/elasti/templates/_helpers.tpl`, `charts/elasti/templates/deployment.yaml` | Adds `elasti.commonEnvValues` helper emitting operator/resolver env vars (names, services, ports, KUBERNETES_CLUSTER_DOMAIN). Injects helper into operator and resolver containers; removes explicit KUBERNETES_CLUSTER_DOMAIN envs. | | **Operator: adopt config package**
`operator/cmd/main.go`, `operator/internal/informer/informer.go`, `operator/internal/controller/opsDeployment.go`, `operator/internal/controller/opsEndpointslices.go`, `operator/internal/controller/opsInformer.go`, `operator/internal/controller/elastiservice_controller.go` | Imports config; replaces hard-coded resolver/operator identifiers and ports with config-driven values; updates informer namespace/name selectors; uses configured reverse proxy port in EndpointSlices; removes unused resolver constants. | | **Config module (new)**
`pkg/config/config.go` | Adds env-driven configuration: Config, ResolverConfig, getters for resolver/operator config and cluster domain; strict parsing with panic on missing/invalid envs; defines env var co

operator/Makefile (1)
`152-152`: **LGTM! Controller-tools version update aligns with PR scope.** The bump from v0.14.0 to v0.19.0 is consistent with the CRD schema changes throughout the PR.

`6-6`: **LGTM! Controller-gen version updated consistently.** The version bump to v0.19.0 matches the Makefile change and other CRD updates in this PR. --- `99-101`: **LGTM! Required fields alignment addresses validation gap.** Based on the past review discussion, these fields were already required at the code level but not enforced in the CRD schema. This change aligns the schema with the actual implementation requirements.
operator/internal/controller/elastiservice_controller_test.go (2)
`25-25`: **LGTM! Test now uses centralized configuration.** The import aligns with the PR's goal of centralizing configuration management. --- `58-62`: **Add missing cluster domain environment variable.** The test should set the cluster domain to prevent panics if reconciliation code begins reading `GetKubernetesClusterDomain()`. Apply this diff to add the missing environment variable:

resolver/internal/operator/RPCClient.go (1)
`38-46`: **Make operator host FQDN-safe and set HTTP client timeout.** - Current code always appends “..svc.” which breaks if ServiceName is already FQDN. - http.Client has no timeout; network issues can hang indefinitely. Apply this diff:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeelasti installed or the issue environment reproducible.
